course_description:
Data science is transforming business and the ways in which the applied business disciplines are practiced. The field of business analytics has emerged as a result of application of the application of data science to business problems. This course introduces students to the fundamental principles of business analytics. The course aims to firstly, provide students with an appreciation of how business analytics are changing business practices and second, to offer a specific overview of key topics in business analytics including predictive modelling.

The success of businesses such as Google, Netflix, and WeChat tells us the world of business is changing and becoming increasingly data-driven. The emergence of specialist consulting firms like Quantium and the development of practice areas in business analytics by the "big four" consulting firms are also emblematic of this shift in business thinking and practice -- reflecting the growing importance of analytics to many businesses. The purpose of the Business Analytics major is to equip students with the data and statistical literacy increasingly required to succeed in modern business environments.

BSAN2201 Principles of Business Analytics introduces you to the core concepts, domains, and real-world applications of analytics. You will learn core analytics concepts, apply appropriate analytical methods to business problems, and assess how analytics supports managerial and strategic decision-making. This course equips you with the essential data literacy and analytical mindset required to succeed in today’s increasingly data-driven business environment.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

course_aims: The broad aim of the course is to provide students with an overview of the domains of business analytics and how the application of analytics to those domains can improve organisational performance. The domains include accounting/financial, people/talent, operations, marketing, social media, and supply chain analytics. Methods of business analytics are previewed -- but they are the focus of the companion course: BSAN2204 Methods of Business Analytics.ﾠ The Principles course has the aim of building understanding of business analytics and its potential applications to business, and understanding the change management processes that might be required to support its successful adoption by organisations.

learning_outcomes.description: Recognise and explain the importance of business analytics to managerial decision making
Explain the basic concepts used in business analytics and their applications to managerial decision making
Identify and apply the appropriate methods of business analytics for solving a range of business problems
Compare and critically evaluate the various approaches to business analytics, and
Evaluate the value of business analytics for improving managerial and strategic decision making.

assessment_details.title: Assessment 1 - Structured Summary and Article Review
assessment_details.due_date: Component 1: Structured Summary (10%) - During Tutorial Week 4,Component 2: Article Review (20%) 24/04/2026 3:00 pm
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment consists of two components: aStructured Summary (10%)and anArticle Review (20%). Component 1 requires students to write a structured summary of a business analytics related article, while Component 2 involves constructing a critical article review on a selected business analytics topic. Further details regarding assessment requirements, format, and style are provided in the Assessment 1 Guide, available on Blackboard, and will also be discussed in class.AI Statement:Artificial Intelligence (AI) provides emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI in completing this assessment task; however, students must clearly reference any use of AI in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description

assessment_details.title: Assessment 2 - Case Study Report and Poster Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: Component 1: Case Study Report (40%) 29/05/2026 3:00 pmComponent 2: Poster Presentation During Tutorial Class (20%) Week 13,
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment 2 comprises two components: aCase Study Report (40%)and aPoster Presentation (20%). The Case Study Report requires students to prepare a business analytics report on a selected business, while the Poster Presentation component involves preparing and presenting a poster based on the content of the Case Study Report. learning_activities.topic: Course Introduction and the Business Analytics FieldThis lecture introduces the BSAN2201 course and assessments, the Business Analytics major/field, and career pathways.Note:There are no tutorials in Week 1. Tutorials will commence from Week 2.Learning outcomes:L01

Understanding Business Analytics and Assessment 1's Component 1This lecture introduces the foundations of business analytics and provides a briefing on Assessment 1's Component 1 (Writing Structured Summary of an Article).Learning outcomes:L01, L02

Course Overview and Guidelines for Assessment 1's Component 1This tutorial provides a brief overview of the course and detailed guidance on Writing Structured Summary of an Article (Assessment 1's Component 1).Learning outcomes:L01, L02
Data for Business AnalyticsThis lecture explains big data concepts, data management processes, and associated ethical and security challenges.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Business Analytics and Q&A Session for Assessment 1's Component 1This tutorial discusses what business analytics is and addresses questions related to Assessment 1's Component 1: Writing a Structured Summary of an Article (Touchpoint 1).Learning outcomes:L01, L02
Methods and Technologies of Business AnalyticsThis lecture examines major business analytical methods and technologies.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Touchpoint 1: Writing Structured Summary of an ArticleThis tutorial serves asTouchpoint 1, during which students will write a structured summary of an article in class (Assessment 1's Component 1).Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Key Statistical Techniques in Business Analytics and Assessment 1's Component 2 BriefingThis lecture discusses key statistical techniques used in business analytics and provides a detailed briefing on Assessment 1's Component 2: Article Review.Note:Please attend this week’s tutorial class to participate in hands-on practice for Assessment 1's Component 2.Learning outcomes:L02, L03, L04

Using GenAI to Construct an Article Review (Assessment 1's Component 2)This tutorial provides hands-on practice in constructing an Article Review (Assessment 1's Component 2) using GenAI.Learning outcomes:L02, L03, L04, L05
The (Business) Analytics AdvantageThis lecture introduces analytical competition and the role of business analytics in creating competitive advantage.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Predictive Analytics with Regression in R/RStudioThis lecture discusses regression analysis as a key predictive analytics technique and introduces R and RStudio.Good Friday Public Holiday - Friday 3 April 2026- There will be no tutorials on Friday this week; however, a recording of this week’s tutorial will be available for students enrolled in Friday tutorials.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05
Mid-Semester Break

Competing on Analytics (Internal Applications)This lecture explores internal applications of business analytics across different business functions to compete in the market.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
The (Business) Analytics AdvantageThis tutorial includes discussion questions on the (business) analytics advantage.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Competing on Analytics (External Applications) and Assessment 2's Component 1 BriefingThis lecture explores various external applications of business analytics that provide firms with a basis to compete in the market. It also provides a briefing on Assessment 2's Component 1.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Competing on Analytics (Internal and External Applications)This tutorial includes discussion questions related to internal and external applications of business analytics.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Developing Analytics CapabilityThis lecture discusses how organisations build analytical capabilities and become analytical competitors.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Using GenAI for Research to Support the Case Study (Assessment 2's Component 1)This tutorial provides a comprehensive guide on using GenAI for research to support Assessment 2's Component 1: Case Study Report.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05
The Business Analytics ProcessThis lecture explores the seven-step analytics process and the role of creativity in analytical thinking.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Developing Analytics Capability/The Business Analytics ProcessThis tutorial addresses discussion questions on developing analytics capability and the business analytics process.Labour Day Public Holiday - Monday 4 May 2026- There will be no tutorials on Monday this week, if any. However, a recording of this week’s tutorial will be available for students enrolled in Monday tutorials.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Assessment 2's Component 2 BriefingThis lecture introduces AI, machine learning, and their applications in business analytics. It also provides guidance on Poster Presentation (Assessment 2's Component 2).Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Poster Preparation and Presentation Guidelines (Assessment 2's Component 2)This tutorial provides guidance on poster preparation and instructions for poster presentation (Assessment 2's Component 2).Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05

Deep Learning and BeyondThis lecture examines deep learning, neural networks, and perspectives on artificial general and super-intelligent AI in business analytics context.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning/Deep Learning and BeyondThis tutorial discusses the roles of AI, machine learning, and deep learning in business analytics.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Revision and Assessment 3 BriefingThis final lecture of the semester provides a brief recap of the course and an overview of Assessment 3: Reflection (Video).Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Touchpoint 2: Poster PresentationThis final tutorial serves asTouchpoint 2, where students will present their posters in their respective tutorial classes (Assessment 2's Component 2).Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
